scenario: Pinnacle Healthcare models a Camunda 8.8 admissions process with one subprocess shape that conceals nested logic for 9000 customers per month. The team wants the diagram to stay readable by hiding internal steps inside one activity shape while keeping the same diagram file.
question: Which statement about a collapsed embedded subprocess in this Camunda 8.8 model is supported by the documentation?

options.text: A collapsed embedded subprocess can reuse a different already deployed process definition through the drill-down link in Modeler from inside the parent diagram of the process
A collapsed embedded subprocess is fully supported by Optimize, including drill-down into the nested elements of the subprocess from the Optimize UI for the parent process
A collapsed embedded subprocess can be created by collapsing an existing expanded embedded subprocess in the same Camunda Modeler diagram of the parent process file
A collapsed embedded subprocess hides its internal details for display only, and the drill-down link in Modeler only opens the same embedded subprocess within the diagram

a.text: Incorrect. The Collapsed subprocesses section states the drill-down link only opens the embedded subprocess within the same diagram; to reference another process, use a call activity instead.

b.text: Incorrect. The page contains a caution that collapsed subprocesses are currently only partially supported by Optimize, and drill-down into them in Optimize is not possible from the UI.

c.text: Incorrect. The page states you cannot collapse an existing expanded subprocess in your model; the collapsed or expanded form is chosen when the subprocess is added to the diagram.

d.text: Correct. The Collapsed subprocesses section states they serve purely display purposes, hiding internal details inside the activity shape, and the drill-down link only opens the same embedded subprocess within the diagram.

explanation: Collapsed embedded subprocesses are a display-only convenience in Modeler. They cannot be created from an already expanded subprocess, they are only partially supported by Optimize, and the drill-down link does not reference a different process. Use a call activity for reuse.
